_note: SAMPLE CONTENT — all articles, statistics, and attributions are illustrative placeholders for design and demonstration purposes only.

articles.title: ICHRA Membership Grows 124% YoY — What It Means for Carriers
articles.summary: New HRA Council data shows ICHRA is no longer a fringe benefit. Enrollment has crossed the million-life threshold and the growth trajectory is steepening. Here's what carrier strategy teams need to know heading into Q2 2026.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team

articles.title: The Carrier Activation Playbook: How Leading Insurers Are Winning ICHRA
articles.summary: The carriers gaining market share in ICHRA aren't the biggest — they're the most connected. A look at the activation moves that separate leaders from laggards: platform partnerships, broker toolkits, and real-time eligibility.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team

articles.title: The Broker Education Gap Is ICHRA's Biggest Growth Barrier
articles.summary: Survey data from 400 independent brokers reveals that 61% cannot explain ICHRA compliantly to an employer group. That's not a product problem — it's a distribution problem. And carriers are uniquely positioned to fix it.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team

articles.title: CMS 2026 ICHRA Guidance: What Changed and What Carriers Must Do
articles.summary: CMS released updated ICHRA affordability and substantiation guidance in February 2026. This summary covers the four changes that directly affect carrier administration, reporting obligations, and employee notice requirements.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team

articles.title: Platform Comparison: Why Carriers Are Choosing HealthSherpa for ICHRA Distribution
articles.summary: Not all ICHRA platforms are built for carrier needs. We break down the key differentiators — enrollment volume, API depth, carrier portal quality, and compliance tooling — across the top platforms currently serving the market.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team

articles.title: How a 200-Person Manufacturer Switched to ICHRA and Cut Benefits Cost 18%
articles.summary: A Midwest manufacturing company with 200 employees moved from a fully-insured group plan to ICHRA in 2025. Twelve months in: 18% cost reduction, 94% employee retention, zero compliance issues. Here's how they did it — and what carriers learned.
articles.author: ICHRAverse Research Team
